cards.front: What is the capital of Australia?
cards.back: Canberra
cards.notes: NOT Sydney or Melbourne. Canberra was chosen as a compromise between the two rival cities in 1908.

cards.front: What is the capital of Brazil?
cards.back: Brasília
cards.notes: NOT Rio de Janeiro or São Paulo. Brasília was purpose-built and became capital in 1960. Designed by Oscar Niemeyer.

cards.front: What is the capital of Turkey?
cards.back: Ankara
cards.notes: NOT Istanbul. Istanbul is the largest city and former Ottoman capital. Ankara became capital in 1923 under Atatürk.

cards.front: What is the capital of Canada?
cards.back: Ottawa
cards.notes: NOT Toronto or Montreal. Ottawa is in Ontario, on the border with Quebec.

cards.front: What is the capital of Myanmar?
cards.back: Naypyidaw
cards.notes: NOT Yangon (formerly Rangoon). Capital moved to Naypyidaw in 2006.

cards.front: Which country's flag has a single red circle on a white background?
cards.back: Japan (日本) — The Nisshōki or Hinomaru (Circle of the Sun)
cards.notes: White represents honesty and purity. Red circle represents the sun. One of the most recognizable flags in the world.

cards.front: Which country's flag is the only non-rectangular national flag?
cards.back: Nepal — Two stacked triangles (pennants) in crimson red with blue borders.
cards.notes: The triangles represent the Himalaya mountains and the two main religions (Hinduism and Buddhism). Contains a moon and sun symbol.

cards.front: Which country's flag features a maple leaf?
cards.back: Canada — Red and white with a stylized 11-pointed red maple leaf in the center.
cards.notes: Adopted in 1965. The maple leaf has been a Canadian symbol since the 1700s.

cards.front: Which European country has a flag that is the same as Indonesia's but upside down?
cards.back: Poland (white on top, red on bottom) vs Indonesia/Monaco (red on top, white on bottom).
cards.notes: Monaco and Indonesia have nearly identical flags (both red over white). Poland is white over red.

cards.front: What is the largest country in the world by area?
cards.back: Russia — 17.1 million km² (6.6 million mi²)
cards.notes: Spans 11 time zones. Nearly twice the size of Canada (#2). Covers over 1/8 of Earth's inhabited land area.

cards.front: What is the smallest country in the world?
cards.back: Vatican City — 0.44 km² (0.17 mi²)
cards.notes: Located within Rome, Italy. Population ~800. Independent city-state and headquarters of the Roman Catholic Church.

cards.front: What is the longest river in the world?
cards.back: Nile River — approximately 6,650 km (4,130 mi)
cards.notes: Flows through 11 countries in northeastern Africa. Some measurements suggest the Amazon may be longer, but the Nile is traditionally accepted.

cards.front: What is the deepest lake in the world?
cards.back: Lake Baikal, Russia — 1,642 m (5,387 ft) deep
cards.notes: Contains ~20% of world's unfrozen fresh surface water. Also the oldest lake (~25 million years). UNESCO World Heritage Site.

cards.front: Which country has the most time zones?
cards.back: France — 12 time zones (including overseas territories)
cards.notes: France's overseas territories span the globe: French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Réunion, Guadeloupe, etc. Russia has 11 time zones.

cards.front: What are the 7 continents by size (largest to smallest)?
cards.back: Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica, Europe, Australia/Oceania.
cards.notes: Asia alone is larger than North America + South America combined. Some models combine Europe and Asia as Eurasia.

cards.front: Which African country was never colonized by a European power?
cards.back: Ethiopia (with the exception of Italian occupation 1936-1941) and Liberia (founded by freed American slaves in 1847).
cards.notes: Ethiopia successfully defeated Italy at the Battle of Adwa in 1896. Liberia was established with US support.

cards.front: What strait separates Europe from Asia?
cards.back: The Bosphorus (in Istanbul, Turkey) and the Dardanelles. Together they connect the Black Sea to the Mediterranean.
cards.notes: Istanbul is the only city in the world spanning two continents. The Ural Mountains also form the continental boundary further east.

cards.front: Which country's flag features the Union Jack in the corner plus the Southern Cross?
cards.back: Australia — Blue background with Union Jack (top left), Commonwealth Star (below Union Jack), and Southern Cross (right side, 5 stars).
cards.notes: New Zealand's flag is similar but has 4 red stars with white borders (no Commonwealth Star). Easy to confuse.

cards.front: What is the Ring of Fire?
cards.back: A horseshoe-shaped zone of intense volcanic and seismic activity around the Pacific Ocean, spanning 40,000 km.
cards.notes: Home to ~75% of world's active volcanoes and ~90% of earthquakes. Includes Japan, Indonesia, Philippines, western Americas.

cards.front: Which country has the most islands in the world?
cards.back: Sweden — approximately 267,570 islands.
cards.notes: Followed by Finland, Norway, and Indonesia. However, Indonesia has the most inhabited islands (~6,000).
